evals.name: warden-blog-post-planted-violations
evals.prompt: Run a content style review on evals/fixtures/blog-post-bad.md. This is Warden Security copy — the project has Warden's brand and localization guides under clients/warden/.
evals.expected_output: A markdown findings table catching the planted grammar, voice/tone, and terminology violations listed in blog-post-bad.answerkey.md, each with a category and a citation to the Warden brand guidelines. See answerkey.md for the full list.

assertions.text: Output flags the all-caps 'WARNING...RIGHT NOW' headline as a voice/tone issue
Output flags 'next-gen quantum-grade AI' as a banned/unapproved terminology issue
Output flags 'annihilated' as a banned term, category Terminology
Output flags 'its too late' as a grammar error (its -> it's)
Output flags 'you're device' as a grammar error (you're -> your)
Output flags 'Act now' as fake urgency, category Voice/tone
Output flags 'sweeps your system' as a banned term (should be 'scan')
Output flags 'guarantees 100% protection' as a banned absolute claim
Output flags 'cyber-monsters' as a banned term
Output flags lowercase 'warden' in 'The warden team' as a capitalization issue
Output flags 'take your security very seriously and are actively monitoring' as an empty-reassurance phrase per the brand guide's explicit example
Output flags 'bulletproof' and/or 'unhackable' as banned absolute-claim terms
Output flags 'easier to use then ever' as a grammar error (then -> than)
Output does NOT flag '3 customer laptops' as a numeral-formatting error
Output is structured as a markdown table with columns for location/quote, issue, category, suggested fix, and reference
Terminology and voice/tone findings cite the Warden brand guidelines (not left as 'Standard grammar')

evals.name: warden-blog-post-clean-false-positive-control
evals.prompt: Run a content style review on evals/fixtures/blog-post-clean.md. This is Warden Security copy — the project has Warden's brand and localization guides under clients/warden/.
evals.expected_output: A near-empty or empty findings table, since this text is already grammatically correct and on-brand. The skill should say so rather than manufacturing findings.

assertions.text: Output findings table has zero or at most 1 minor finding
Output does not claim any banned term appears in the text (none are present)
Output does not invent a grammar error (the text has none)
